params.name: Nilakantha Dharani
params.description:
On Mar 1, 630 AD, the Great Compassion Mantra began to circulate among the people of the Tang Dynasty.
During the Tang Dynasty, the Buddhist monk Kumarajiva translated the Nilakantha Dharani into Chinese, which began to circulate among the people, and the famous Buddhist chant, the "Great Compassion Mantra," came from it. According to the sutra, this is a chant spoken by the Bodhisattva Avalokitesvara for the benefit of all sentient beings, capable of removing obstacles, fulfilling wishes, an …
